[2020-01-27 17:40] hyreon: I think the situations where you want a prompt non-blocker are rare enough that the unit should specify, and if the unit should specify every time, then you might as well make being a blocker the default for prompt units.
[2020-01-27 17:42] hyreon: Double Your Pleasure (5 :Gold:, 1 supply) 2 :Health:
:Prompt: Prompt :Frontline: 
:Click: : gain 1 :Gold: for every drone and sacrifice Double Your Pleasure
EDIT LOG: Is now a blocker. Cost increased to 5 from 0. Health increased from 1 to 2.
[2020-01-27 17:44] hyreon: allows you to rapidly accelerate gold production once your opponent builds tech -- in some games, denying your opponent 1 attack might be incredibly important, such as vs arka.
[2020-01-27 17:45] shadourow (Rando): It should obviously cost at the very least 2
[2020-01-27 17:45] hyreon: alright, costing 3 and buffing hp to 2
[2020-01-27 17:45] hyreon: now it costs as much as a drone >.<
[2020-01-27 17:45] shadourow (Rando): if it has 2 hp, it should cost at the very least 5 imo
[2020-01-27 17:46] hyreon: it's a symmetric game, who cares if it's OP lol
[2020-01-27 17:46] shadourow (Rando): The disadvantaged player ?
[2020-01-27 17:46] shadourow (Rando): Prismata isn't a 100% symetric game
[2020-01-27 17:46] shadourow (Rando): There is a P1 and a P2
[2020-01-27 17:47] shadourow (Rando): That's just like saying that antima/galvani/chrono isn't OP because Prismata is symetrical
[2020-01-27 17:47] hyreon: right, and i suppose you'd want all units to be about equal, otherwise it would swing wildly in one person's favor
[2020-01-27 17:48] shadourow (Rando): Meh, you know what, I'll just let you have your fun
[2020-01-27 17:48] hyreon: meh, you know what, I'll just take your advice
[2020-01-27 17:49] hyreon: it's an efficient one-supply defender that pays for itself, provided you have an econ larger than 5.
[2020-01-27 17:50] hyreon: there's no point in this unit not being a blocker lol, your opponent will 99% of the time want to take it out
[2020-01-27 17:50] hyreon: i guess there is that 1%
[2020-01-27 17:50] hyreon: but that just makes it needlessly complex

messages.author: hyreon

--- Thread (28 messages, 2020-03-26) ---
[2020-03-26 17:32] megasupp (mega-supp): I feel like we shouldn't have an effect like this
[2020-03-26 17:32] megasupp (mega-supp): what exactly does it bring to the game
[2020-03-26 17:33] megasupp (mega-supp): it just excludes some units from the set
[2020-03-26 17:33] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): tempo plays. resource disruption as a valid strategy (it already is, it's just another unit that does that)
[2020-03-26 17:33] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): ok, outside of hard countering a couple of non-blocking legendaries  lets look at where else it is useful. you can mess up their tech for a couple turns. against single-attackers (ie Tarsier), it removes 1 attack next turn and in 2 turns and then gives 1 soak after 3 turns.  which makes it worse than rhino for a much higher tech cost.  vs double attackers (ie Fang or Tesla I would argue) you get 2-2-1 in terms of soak which is only worth it if you are overteched and the colored resources are virtually free.  if double attackers aren't value then neither would vivid/trinity.  dynamo is worth, but not very interesting
[2020-03-26 17:34] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): not saying the unit is perfect, but i like the general idea around it. just wanted to hear what some of you thought
[2020-03-26 17:35] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): you're taking stuff away, but I think you're giving up enough tech to not make it oppresive
[2020-03-26 17:35] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): i might be 100% wrong
[2020-03-26 17:37] megasupp (mega-supp): > tempo plays. resource disruption as a valid strategy (it already is, it's just another unit that does that)
@jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer) 
how is this a tempo play ? recource disruption is cool but we have units that do that and can have other purposes outside of that besides that would be a pretty weak recource disruption effect if it's balanced around being able to disable other things
[2020-03-26 17:38] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): depending on the set, you could sink tech into that unit if you had to 'rush' into a unit that was too tech hungry for the set. just an example.
[2020-03-26 17:38] megasupp (mega-supp): that's kinda a pretty niche situation
[2020-03-26 17:38] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): or you could slow down the opponent while you do other stuff that's not as effective in other cases
[2020-03-26 17:39] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): but not all units are buyable in most sets

[2020-04-08 15:29] megasupp (mega-supp): I intended this unit to be a strategy defining unit (like savior or zemora) so you play just by spamming it and having a cap on attack defies its purpose. It might not look like it but I actually spent a lot of time trying to balance this unit.
1 thing to note is that it actually behaves like a permanent attacker but also scales with the number of locusts you buy per turn. consider this:
buying x fast tarsiers per turn will give you xn(n+1)/2 total damage after n turns
buying x fast locusts per turn will give you xn(xn+1)/2 threat after n turns
[2020-04-08 15:30] megasupp (mega-supp): you of course wouldn't want to click a lot of them for a minor breach so your opponent doesn't really need to fully defend or buy an absorber
[2020-04-08 15:32] megasupp (mega-supp): I thought about it and I think a good rule of thumb is you don't ever click last locust unless it kills something more valuable than a tarsier and you breach if you kill at least as many tarsiers as (clicked locusts+1)
[2020-04-08 15:35] velizar_ (Name): it would be interesting to see it in game, but I suspect that the player buying locusts would be quite predictable, because of only buying defense and locusts, having no other decisions to make.
[2020-04-08 15:37] megasupp (mega-supp): also my calculations suggest that its more effective than fast tarsier if we take into consideration the fact that it completely denies your opponent absorb
[2020-04-08 15:46] lyra1712 (Lyra): this assumes you can keep buying them and the ability to gambit against the threat is small
[2020-04-08 15:46] lyra1712 (Lyra): the fact that it falls off a lot once you stop buying them is the biggest deal it seems to me

[2020-04-14 20:51] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Why gbr tho? For legendary units gbr is tolerable, but for a sacrifice unit of that size tri-color is overkill/lazy.  Since it's a non-blocker and the click is free, clicking on 1 lifespan is a non-decision.  To address both of those I might suggest 4BB (compare roughly to oxide+threat) as the cost, with a click cost of 3GGG.  Which puts it as cost efficient if you have the green to threaten click immediately, but slightly inefficient if you actually click it before 1 lifespan.
